_meta.description: Sample DevOps-agent configurations for the Agent Constraint Triangle (Ch1). Each config describes an agent by its reasoning-chain length, tool-catalog size and disambiguity, and context budget vs. usage. Fictional AWS account 123456789012.
_meta.source: Agentic GraphRAG Ch1 — The Agent Constraint Triangle (complexity management / tool orchestration / context utilization)

configs.name: reactive-runbook
configs.note: Early reactive DevOps agent: short scripted chains, few well-named tools, small prompt.

configs.name: bloated-mcp-agent
configs.note: The Block/Goose failure shape: every MCP server enabled 'just in case', overlapping tools, prompt near budget.

configs.name: deep-investigation-agent
configs.note: Long multi-hop incident investigation: many steps, curated disambiguable tools, heavy context.

configs.name: balanced-graph-agent
configs.note: Target shape: bounded steps, RAG-MCP-filtered disambiguable tools, selective context retention.
